title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: When Suhayl saw this, he was furious at the thought of his escape from imprisonment! He then shouted at the top of his voice, "This man, O Muhammad, is the ﬁrst person I demand that you send back to me!" The Prophet said, "We haven't ﬁnalised the pact, yet." Suhayl said, "In that case, by Allah, I will not sign any pact with you!" The Prophet said, "Then allow me to take him." He said, "I will not allow you to take him." The Prophet said, "Indeed, you will!" He said, "No, I will not!" The Prophet then remained silent. He was very eager to bring Quraysh closer to Islam however much he could. He did Help by your speech if you cannot do so in any other way not wish that one Muslim's plight would have an adverse affect on the entire pact. Suhayl quickly got hold of his son and began to drag him along by his chains as Abu Jandal was shouting, begging the Muslims for help. He said, "O Muslims! Am I going to be returned to the pagans after I have come to you as a Muslim?! Do you not see the ordeal I have undergone?" He continued to call for help until he eventually disappeared from their sight. The Muslims' hearts were ﬁlled with grief over him. They saw a young man who was being tortured − the son of an elite ﬁgure who had left a life of ease for punishment and torture. They helplessly watched him dragged on the ground in chains in front of them to be returned to his prison. Abu Jandal thus went back to Makkah, asking Allah to make him steadfast on his religion, to protect him, and to grant him further conviction in his beliefs. The Muslims returned to Madinah with the Messenger of Allah , their hearts enraged by the disbelievers and at the same time pitiful for the weak and oppressed Muslims. Thereafter, the oppression against the Muslims increased to an extent that they were unable to bear it any longer. Abu Jandal, his friend Abu Baseer and other oppressed Muslims decided to plan an escape for themselves. Abu Baseer managed to escape and instantly headed for Madinah, hoping to be in the company of the Messenger of Allah and his Companions. He continued to walk in the heat of the desert until he ﬁnally reached Madinah and headed for the Mosque. As the Prophet was sitting with his companions, there came to them Abu Baseer Help by your speech if you cannot do so in any other way upon whom the affects of torture were quite visible. He looked exhausted from his journey. He had not even managed regain his breath when two men from the disbelievers of Quraysh arrived and entered the Mosque. When Abu Baseer saw them, he became terriﬁed and began recalling the torture he had undergone. The two men entered shouting, "O Muhammad! Return him to us according to the pact you signed with us!" The Prophet remembered the pact he had signed with the Quraysh where he had agreed to return to them whoever comes to him from Makkah. He ordered Abu Baseer to leave Madinah, and so he did with the two disbelievers. When they exited Madinah, they sat down to eat. One of them sat with Abu Baseer while the other disappeared to relieve himself. The one sitting next to Abu Baseer took his sword out and began to play with it. He said to Abu Baseer jokingly, "I will strike with this sword al-Aws and al-Khazraj all day long!" Abu Baseer said to him, "By Allah, I see your sword is excellent!" He said, "Yes, by Allah! This sword is ﬁne, I have used it over and over!" Abu Baseer said, "Allow me to have a look, please." The man gave him the sword. As soon as he got hold of it, he raised the sword and struck his neck with it, causing his head to ﬂy off.When the man's companion came back and saw the body of his friend cut up, he was terriﬁed and rushed to Madinah to the Prophet in his Mosque. When the Prophet saw him terriﬁed, he said, "He must Help by your speech if you cannot do so in any other way have seen something awful." He stood in front of the Prophet and screamed, "By Allah, my friend has been killed and I am going to be killed, too!" It wasn't long before Abu Baseer entered the Mosque, looking sinister with a blood-stained sword in his hand. He said, "O Prophet of Allah! Allah has fulﬁlled your trust. You did return me to them, but Allah has saved me. Please, accept me!" The Prophet said, "No." Abu Baseer then said in a loud voice, "OK then, give me a man and I will conquer Makkah for you!" The Prophet was amazed at his valour.
content_id: Ketika Suhayl melihat ini, ia murka atas pikiran tentang pelarian putranya dari penjara! Ia kemudian berteriak sekeras-kerasnya, "Orang ini, wahai Muhammad, adalah orang pertama yang aku tuntut agar engkau kembalikan kepadaku!" Nabi berkata, "Kami belum menyelesaikan perjanjian." Suhayl berkata, "Dalam hal itu, demi Allah, aku tidak akan menandatangani perjanjian apa pun denganmu!" Nabi berkata, "Kalau begitu, izinkan aku untuk membawanya." Ia berkata, "Aku tidak akan mengizinkanmu membawanya." Nabi berkata, "Sungguh, engkau akan mengizinkannya!" Ia berkata, "Tidak, aku tidak akan!" Nabi kemudian terdiam. Beliau sangat ingin membawa Quraisy lebih dekat kepada Islam sebanyak mungkin. Beliau tidak menginginkan penderitaan satu Muslim akan berdampak buruk pada seluruh perjanjian. Suhayl segera memegang anaknya dan mulai menyeretnya dengan rantai sementara Abu Jandal berteriak, memohon bantuan dari kaum Muslimin. Ia berkata, "Wahai kaum Muslimin! Akankah aku dikembalikan kepada orang-orang kafir setelah aku datang kepada kalian sebagai seorang Muslim?! Tidakkah kalian melihat cobaan yang aku alami?" Ia terus meminta bantuan hingga akhirnya ia menghilang dari pandangan mereka. Hati kaum Muslimin dipenuhi kesedihan karenanya. Mereka melihat seorang pemuda yang sedang disiksa - putra dari seorang tokoh terkemuka yang telah meninggalkan kehidupan nyaman demi hukuman dan siksaan. Mereka dengan tak berdaya menyaksikannya diseret di tanah dengan rantai di hadapan mereka untuk dikembalikan ke penjaranya. Abu Jandal pun kembali ke Makkah, memohon kepada Allah agar membuatnya teguh dalam agamanya, melindunginya, dan memberikannya keyakinan yang lebih kuat dalam kepercayaannya. Kaum Muslimin kembali ke Madinah bersama Rasulullah, dengan hati yang dipenuhi kemarahan terhadap orang-orang kafir dan sekaligus rasa kasihan terhadap kaum Muslimin yang lemah dan tertindas. Setelah itu, penindasan terhadap kaum Muslimin semakin meningkat hingga mereka tidak tahan lagi. Abu Jandal, temannya Abu Bashir, dan kaum Muslimin yang tertindas lainnya memutuskan untuk merencanakan pelarian bagi diri mereka sendiri. Abu Bashir berhasil melarikan diri dan langsung menuju Madinah, berharap dapat berada dalam kebersamaan Rasulullah dan para Sahabatnya. Ia terus berjalan dalam terik padang pasir hingga akhirnya tiba di Madinah dan menuju Masjid. Ketika Nabi sedang duduk bersama para sahabatnya, datanglah kepada mereka Abu Bashir yang tampak jelas bekas-bekas siksaan pada dirinya. Ia terlihat kelelahan akibat perjalanannya. Ia bahkan belum sempat memulihkan nafasnya ketika dua orang dari orang-orang kafir Quraisy tiba dan memasuki Masjid. Ketika Abu Bashir melihat mereka, ia menjadi ketakutan dan mulai mengingat kembali siksaan yang pernah ia alami. Kedua orang itu masuk sambil berteriak, "Wahai Muhammad! Kembalikan dia kepada kami sesuai perjanjian yang telah engkau tandatangani bersama kami!" Nabi mengingat perjanjian yang telah ia tandatangani dengan Quraisy di mana ia telah setuju untuk mengembalikan kepada mereka siapapun yang datang kepadanya dari Makkah. Beliau memerintahkan Abu Bashir untuk meninggalkan Madinah, dan ia pun pergi bersama dua orang kafir itu. Ketika mereka keluar dari Madinah, mereka duduk untuk makan. Salah satu dari mereka duduk bersama Abu Bashir sementara yang lain pergi untuk buang hajat. Yang duduk di samping Abu Bashir mengeluarkan pedangnya dan mulai memainkannya. Ia berkata kepada Abu Bashir dengan bercanda, "Aku akan menyerang al-Aws dan al-Khazraj sepanjang hari dengan pedang ini!" Abu Bashir berkata kepadanya, "Demi Allah, aku melihat pedangmu sangat bagus!" Ia berkata, "Ya, demi Allah! Pedang ini hebat, aku telah menggunakannya berkali-kali!" Abu Bashir berkata, "Izinkan aku melihatnya sebentar." Pria itu memberikannya. Begitu ia memegang pedang itu, ia mengangkat pedang dan memukul lehernya, menyebabkan kepalanya terpenggal. Ketika teman pria itu kembali dan melihat tubuh temannya terpotong, ia ketakutan dan berlari menuju Madinah kepada Nabi di Masjidnya. Ketika Nabi melihatnya dalam keadaan ketakutan, beliau berkata, "Ia pasti telah melihat sesuatu yang mengerikan." Ia berdiri di hadapan Nabi dan berteriak, "Demi Allah, temanku telah terbunuh dan aku pun akan terbunuh!" Tidak lama kemudian Abu Bashir memasuki Masjid dengan tampak mengerikan membawa pedang berlumuran darah di tangannya. Ia berkata, "Wahai Nabi Allah! Allah telah memenuhi kepercayaanmu. Engkau memang telah mengembalikanku kepada mereka, tetapi Allah telah menyelamatkanku. Tolong, terimalah aku!" Nabi berkata, "Tidak." Abu Bashir kemudian berkata dengan suara keras, "Baiklah, beri aku seorang lelaki dan aku akan menaklukkan Makkah untukmu!" Nabi kagum dengan keberaniannya.
content_ar: يختتم المؤلف كتابه "استمتع بحياتك" بمزيد من المواقف والحكايات المُستقاة من السيرة النبوية الشريفة وسير الصحابة الكرام. ويؤكد أهمية حسن الخلق والتواضع والإخلاص في التعامل مع الناس، مُختتمًا بنصائح عملية لمن يريد أن يعيش حياةً سعيدة مع نفسه ومع مجتمعه.
